focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Carl begins as a lonely boy shaped by idolization of adventure, only to find true purpose not in distant waterfalls but in the quiet, shared life he builds with Ellie—whose loss leaves him clinging to the past until a journey forces him to confront that the greatest adventure was love itself; now, adrift in sky and spirit, he finally releases the house that held his grief, choosing connection over memory as he laughs, exhausted but alive, in the embrace of a new family.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: central observer and emotional focal point; young Carl is introduced as an ardent admirer of Muntz and adventure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl internalizes Charles Muntz's ideals and adopts his adventurous spirit, symbolically aligning himself with Muntz through mimicry and emotional investment
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl aspires to become a true adventurer like Muntz, inspired by the promise of discovery and heroism
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl's worldview is shaped around the belief in adventure and the righteousness of Muntz's quest, despite public skepticism
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The controversy over the authenticity of the Monster of Paradise Falls skeleton introduces doubt that will later challenge Carl's beliefs
evidence_quotes: In the theater, Young Carl returns the thumbs up.
No!
Carl GASPS.
In the theater, young Carl smiles.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Carl is the focal character encountering Ellie, whose presence disrupts his solitary imaginative world and initiates a transformative relationship.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl loses his balloon—a symbol of his solitary dream—when startled by Ellie, marking the end of his isolated play and the beginning of shared adventure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl's initial goal of pursuing individual exploration and adventure is challenged by Ellie's intrusion and invitation to join her imaginative world.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl transitions from isolation to connection, accepting Ellie's invitation to co-explore, symbolized by her giving him a club pin and taking his hand.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's ability to fully engage verbally and emotionally in this new relationship remains uncertain, as he is initially frozen and embarrassed.
evidence_quotes: Alright, you're in. Welcome aboard.
You and me, we're in a club now.
Carl smiles.
Carl blushes as she pulls him out of the room.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Carl is the recipient of Ellie's trust and the confidant to her dream, marking the beginning of their shared journey.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ellie reveals her Adventure Book and dreams of Paradise Falls to Carl, formally inviting him into her world and securing his promise to fulfill the adventure together.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl adopts Ellie's dream of reaching Paradise Falls as his own, transforming it into a shared mission.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl is emotionally invested in a future adventure he had never imagined, shifting from a quiet observer to a committed partner in Ellie's vision.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The practical challenge of how they will actually reach Paradise Falls remains unanswered, leaving the dream idealistic and untested.
evidence_quotes: My Adventure Book!
Good. You promised. No backing out.
Wow.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl marries Ellie, marking his transition from childhood to adulthood and the formalization of their lifelong partnership.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl and Ellie commit to each other, solidifying their shared dream of reaching Paradise Falls as a joint life goal.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl fully embraces his new identity as Ellie's husband and equal partner in adventure, anchoring his future motivations in their relationship.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl fully embraces a new identity as Ellie's husband and partner in adventure.
evidence_quotes: She jumps at him and gives him a big kiss.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl and Ellie jointly mark the mailbox with their names and handprints, transforming it into a shared symbol of love and partnership.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: The house and mailbox represent the beginning of their life together and embody their shared dreams.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The mailbox becomes a physical manifestation of Carl and Ellie's commitment, publicly affirming their bond and domestic happiness.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Joyful expression of domestic happiness and shared dreams.
evidence_quotes: Ellie finishes painting "Carl & Ellie" on their MAILBOX.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl shares a tender, imaginative moment with Ellie, deepening their emotional intimacy as they lie together watching clouds.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To be present and emotionally connected with Ellie in a moment of shared imagination and peace
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl experiences profound contentment and emotional fulfillment in Ellie's presence, reinforcing the strength of their bond.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Highlights Carl's contentment and emotional fulfillment in being with Ellie.
evidence_quotes: He watches as a cloud transforms into a turtle.
Carl closes his eyes and smiles. He's lucky to be with her.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Carl is the emotional anchor for Ellie during a moment of profound loss, transitioning from hopeful partner to supportive caregiver.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl and Ellie learn they cannot have children, shattering their shared dream and marking a pivotal emotional turning point in their relationship and individual identities.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: The shared goal of starting a family is definitively ended by medical reality.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl begins to internalize grief, shifting toward emotional protection and deepened commitment to Ellie, laying the foundation for his later emotional withdrawal.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The couple does not yet know how to rebuild their future without children, leaving their long-term emotional recovery uncertain.
evidence_quotes: Carl touches Ellie's shoulder as the doctor explains.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 24、INT. CARL AND ELLIE'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl and Ellie create a symbolic shrine to their shared dream of adventure by displaying meaningful artifacts beneath a mural of their house at Paradise Falls.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: the shrine embodies their commitment to the dream of reaching Paradise Falls and solidifies it as a central part of their life together.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: the living room becomes a dedicated space that visually and emotionally reinforces their shared identity and mission.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: domestic ritual reinforces emotional investment in Paradise Falls
evidence_quotes: Ellie paints a MURAL of their house atop Paradise Falls over
the mantle. Carl organizes a compass, map, binoculars, and
native bird figurine beneath the painting. It's their shrine

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 25、INT. CARL AND ELLIE'S HOUSE, LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl and Ellie repeatedly sacrifice their Paradise Falls savings to cover urgent life expenses, symbolizing the erosion of their shared dream due to external hardships.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: the explicit goal of reaching Paradise Falls is deferred indefinitely
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: the 'PARADISE FALLS' jar is broken and repurposed for practical needs, marking the abandonment of the dream in favor of mutual care and survival
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the dream of Paradise Falls remains emotionally significant but unattainable, creating a lasting emotional void that shapes Carl's later actions
evidence_quotes: Carl sets A JAR on a table, "PARADISE FALLS" written on it.
The jar slowly fills as Carl and Ellie toss in spare change.
Their car blows a tire.
Breaking jar.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 28、INT. CARL AND ELLIE'S HOUSE, FRONT HALL - 30 YEARS LATER
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Older Carl shares a quiet, reflective moment with Ellie, acknowledging their shared life and aging together with a smile in the mirror.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To share a moment of mutual recognition and affection with Ellie, reflecting on their life together
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl and Ellie experience a moment of mutual contentment and emotional closeness, reflecting on their enduring bond after decades together.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The passage of time and the inevitability of loss are implied, foreshadowing Carl's future grief.
evidence_quotes: Older Carl and Ellie smile at themselves in the hall mirror.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl transitions from passive remembrance of Ellie and their unfulfilled dream to making a decisive internal commitment to act.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To honor Ellie's memory by fulfilling their shared dream of reaching Paradise Falls.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl forms a concrete plan to revive the dream, marking the beginning of his active journey.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The practical challenges of how to achieve the dream—especially as an elderly man—are not yet addressed.
evidence_quotes: Carl vacuums the Adventure Shrine on the mantle.
Carl has an idea.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ellie collapses due to a sudden health crisis, disrupting Carl's plans and shifting his focus entirely to her well-being.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl's goal shifts from pursuing their shared adventure dream to prioritizing Ellie's immediate health and safety.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl abandons his plan to surprise Ellie with tickets, choosing instead to rush to her aid, signaling a pivotal moment in their relationship and journey.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The nature and severity of Ellie's health issue remain unclear, creating uncertainty about their future together.
evidence_quotes: He runs to her.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl returns to the house alone, physically embodying the loss of Ellie and the beginning of his solitary journey.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl's immediate goal shifts from sharing life with Ellie to preserving her memory and confronting his grief.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl is now fully alone in the home he once shared with Ellie, facing emotional isolation and the weight of his memories.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Carl will move forward with his life and honor Ellie's legacy remains uncertain.
evidence_quotes: Carl walks into the house, holding a single blue balloon.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: protagonist defending his home and connection to Ellie
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl defiantly rejects the developer's increased offer and uses the leaf blower to assert control, symbolically defending his home and marriage.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl is determined to protect his house as a sacred space tied to his life with Ellie.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The external pressure from the developer intensifies, but Carl's resolve to hold onto the house is solidified through his defiant actions.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The developer continues to pursue acquiring Carl's property, posing an ongoing threat to his home and emotional sanctuary.
evidence_quotes: Quite a sight, huh Ellie?
Shady Oaks Retirement. Oh brother.
You poured prune juice in his gas tank.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Carl reluctantly engages with Russell after persistent intrusion, marking the beginning of his forced reconnection with the outside world
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl's isolation is breached both by Russell's persistence and the destruction of Ellie's mailbox, triggering emotional reactivity and unintended engagement
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl initially aims to maintain isolation and reject all external contact, especially from Russell
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl shifts from passive resistance to active, if manipulative, engagement with Russell and then reacts violently to the threat against a symbol of his past, violating his own desire to be left alone
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's violent outburst and retreat into the house leave his emotional state and next actions uncertain; the conflict with the construction crew and real estate developer remains unresolved
evidence_quotes: Carl swings the door shut, but a small camping boot stops it.
Ow.
Carl opens the door. Russell stands at attention.
Proceed.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl is formally dismissed by society as a public nuisance and handed a pamphlet for Shady Oaks Retirement Village, marking the moment he confronts his isolation and obsolescence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl's goal to remain in his home and honor Ellie's memory through passive resistance collapses under institutional pressure.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl enters a state of emotional crisis, questioning his future and signaling the end of passive endurance and the beginning of potential transformation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl does not know how to continue fulfilling his promise to Ellie while facing forced removal from his home.
evidence_quotes: She hands him a pamphlet for SHADY OAKS RETIREMENT VILLAGE.
Carl faces the house. He touches Ellie's dented mailbox.
What do I do now, Ellie?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl reaffirms his commitment to Ellie's dream by symbolically crossing his heart, choosing to honor their shared promise over submitting to external pressures of institutionalization.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To fulfill the promise he made to Ellie by going to Paradise Falls.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl resolves to take active control of his life and destiny, rejecting passive resignation and the prospect of moving to Shady Oaks.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The practical challenges of how to leave and carry out the journey remain unclear.
evidence_quotes: He holds the Shady Oaks pamphlet.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Carl fakes compliance with relocation, then launches his house via balloons.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl deceives the nurses by appearing to cooperate with relocation, then immediately initiates his escape by launching his house into the sky with thousands of balloons.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To fulfill his promise to Ellie by traveling to Paradise Falls, preserving their shared dream.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl successfully breaks free from societal control and begins his journey, transforming from a passive widower into an active adventurer.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The practical challenges of navigating the airborne house and reaching Paradise Falls remain unknown.
evidence_quotes: Carl hands over his suitcase.
The house rips away from its foundation.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Carl is the sole active agent in the scene, transforming his house into a flying vessel and piloting it with purpose.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: The house lifts into the air, now a functional and steerable craft, marking Carl's transition from passive widower to active adventurer.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl achieves control over the house's flight, enacting his long-deferred journey.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The house is airborne and under Carl's command, symbolizing liberation from stagnation and the beginning of a new chapter.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The success of the journey and Carl's ability to sustain this new role remain uncertain.
evidence_quotes: windows and billow in the wind. Carl steers using ropes

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl emotionally commits to the journey by addressing Ellie's photo and formally initiating the flight, marking the transition from preparation to action.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: The journey shifts from a planned endeavor to an actively realized dream, rooted in Carl's promise to Ellie.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl achieves a state of peaceful resolve, embracing his role as an adventurer and honoring his shared past with Ellie.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl transitions from anticipation to peaceful resolve, indicating internal acceptance of his new role as an adventurer.
evidence_quotes: Carl kisses a photo of Ellie.
We're on our way, Ellie.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl reluctantly allows Russell into his home, marking the first breach of his self-imposed isolation since Ellie's death.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl's goal to remain isolated and undisturbed is directly challenged by Russell's unexpected appearance and persistence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Despite initial resistance, Carl permits Russell to enter, initiating a reluctant caretaker role and the first meaningful human connection in his life post-Ellie.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's deeper emotional resistance to companionship remains; his softening is minimal and begrudging, suggesting ongoing internal conflict.
evidence_quotes: What are you doing out here, kid?
No.
Carl slams the door, leaving Russell alone.
Oh, alright, you can come...

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl discovers Ellie's message in the Adventure Book, realizing that their ordinary life together was the true adventure she cherished.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl's mission shifts from fulfilling a physical promise to honoring Ellie's spirit by embracing new connections and experiences.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl begins to let go of grief and isolation, opening himself to future adventures and emotional growth.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl must now learn how to move forward without Ellie, navigating a new life defined by connection rather than duty.
evidence_quotes: The blank pages at the end are no longer blank.
A WEDDING PHOTO of the two of them.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Carl is the active rescuer in the climactic moment of freeing Kevin, fulfilling his primary mission objective.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl opens the cage and physically liberates Kevin, completing the rescue that has driven his journey.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Rescue Kevin from captivity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Kevin is free from the cage and under Carl's protection, transitioning the mission to escape
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The threat of recapture remains as they are still in hostile territory aboard the dirigible
evidence_quotes: Carl opens the cage door.
CARL
I'm sorry Kevin. Let's get you out

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl rescues Russell, Dug, and Kevin, then emotionally lets go of his house—the symbol of his past—embracing his new family with laughter and physical affection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Carl has achieved emotional closure by saving his new loved ones and releasing his obsession with the house.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl accepts that his life's meaning no longer lies in preserving the past, but in nurturing his newfound family and relationships.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl endures physical exhaustion and emotional vulnerability after the rescue.
evidence_quotes: Carl LAUGHS, relieved.
Carl smiles. He puts his arm around Russell.
You know, it's just a house.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Once a celebrated explorer whose fall from grace ignited an obsessive quest to prove his legacy, Charles Muntz descends from charismatic idol to isolated tyrant, his mission warped by pride into a violent, self-consuming pursuit of the bird he believes will restore his honor; ultimately, he is undone not by external judgment but by his own unraveling, meeting his end entangled in the very symbols of his ambition—his dirigible and balloons—while the creatures he commanded choose joy over obedience, leaving his legacy in ruins.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: idolized explorer and public figure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charles Muntz is publicly celebrated as a heroic adventurer and inventor, but then accused of fraud and stripped of his honors, leading him to vow a permanent return to Paradise Falls to prove his credibility.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz's goal is to validate his discovery of the Monster of Paradise Falls by capturing the creature alive to restore his reputation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz transitions from a decorated, admired explorer to a disgraced outcast determined to redeem himself through a solitary, high-stakes mission.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The authenticity of the Monster of Paradise Falls remains in question, casting doubt on Muntz's integrity and setting up his future descent into obsession.
evidence_quotes: Adventure is out there!
And I will not come back until I do!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: symbolic figure of adventure and exploration
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charles Muntz's newspaper clippings in Ellie's room establish him as an iconic explorer, shaping the imaginative play and shared dreams of Ellie and Carl.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz's legacy inspires Ellie and Carl's sense of adventure and becomes foundational to their emotional bond.
evidence_quotes: Carl is distracted by the Muntz newspaper clippings taped to
YOUNG ELLIE (O.S.)

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Symbolic inspiration and narrative catalyst for Ellie's lifelong dream.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charles Muntz's image in Ellie's Adventure Book serves as the foundational inspiration for her dream of traveling to Paradise Falls, establishing him as a heroic figure whose legacy motivates the central mission of the film.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ellie adopts the goal of journeying to Paradise Falls in South America, directly influenced by Charles Muntz's explorations.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl becomes emotionally invested in Ellie's dream after witnessing her passion, leading him to promise to take her to Paradise Falls in a blimp.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ellie does not know how she will reach Paradise Falls, creating a logistical and emotional gap that remains unfulfilled until later in the story.
evidence_quotes: You know him.
It's like America... but south.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charles Muntz transitions from a legendary, off-screen figure to a physically present antagonist, shifting the narrative dynamic by materializing as both an object of admiration and a source of threat.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz's charismatic entrance disarms Carl and Russell, masking his true intentions behind humor and charm, while the surrounding dogs—initially threatening—mirror his control and authority.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz presents himself as hospitable and curious, claiming the encounter is a misunderstanding and offering welcome, but his real goal is to assess and contain Carl and Russell as potential threats to his mission.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl shifts from lifelong idolization of Muntz to a fragile trust, believing he has met his hero, while Russell remains unaware of Muntz's significance, preserving narrative irony.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Muntz's true motives regarding the bird and his willingness to use force remain hidden beneath his affable exterior, creating suspense about his next actions.
evidence_quotes: You came here in that?
Well, you're a man of good taste!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: performative host and self-mythologizing explorer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz constructs an elaborate persona through curated artifacts and embellished stories, manipulating perception to maintain his image as a heroic adventurer.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To impress and gain the trust of Carl and Russell, reinforcing his fabricated legacy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl's admiration for Muntz intensifies, while Russell is entertained; Muntz successfully projects authority and charm, though subtle cues undermine his credibility.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The dogs' mechanical behavior and Muntz's exaggerated tales raise unspoken doubts about the truth behind his mission.
evidence_quotes: Carl and Russell are speechless as they walk into the room.
Various servant dogs dust and polish as Muntz leads Carl and
Muntz and Carl LAUGH. Alpha interrupts.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Confronting his dogs after their failure to capture the bird and discovering Dug's betrayal.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz discovers Dug's betrayal and shifts focus to tracking the protagonists via radar, marking a turning point in his pursuit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To maintain control over his pack and recapture the bird without interference.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz becomes aware of Dug's alliance with the protagonists and initiates active pursuit using radar technology.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Dug's betrayal undermines Muntz's authority and introduces a new threat within his own ranks.
evidence_quotes: You lost them?
Wait. Wait a minute. Dug...
He leans over a RADAR TRACKING DEVICE.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charles Muntz successfully captures Kevin the bird and boards his dirigible, marking the culmination of his long-standing mission.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz achieves his primary objective of capturing the rare bird, transitioning from pursuer to victor preparing for triumphant return.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The protagonists are left defeated and emotionally shattered, with Kevin taken and Carl's house damaged by fire.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The protagonists' determination to continue to Paradise Falls suggests impending confrontation despite Muntz's current victory.
evidence_quotes: Muntz follows the bird up the ramp of the dirigible.
Russell runs after the dogs, but the gangplank closes.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz's sense of triumph is disrupted by the return of Carl, reigniting the central conflict and exposing the fragility of his control.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prove the existence of the rare bird to the scientific community and validate his life's work.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Shifts from confident captor to a figure under renewed threat, as his isolation and obsession begin to undermine his stability.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Muntz can maintain control over the bird and his mission in the face of returning opposition.
evidence_quotes: What?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Antagonist confronting Russell to locate Carl Fredricksen
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz captures Russell and uses him as leverage to draw out Carl Fredricksen, escalating the conflict.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz aims to prevent Carl from interfering with his mission by capturing Russell to force Carl's appearance.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell is captured and taken by Muntz, confirming Carl's imminent approach and heightening the threat level.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl Fredricksen's response to Russell's capture remains unknown, creating tension about the impending confrontation.
evidence_quotes: Where's your elderly friend?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charles Muntz is the antagonist preparing for the climax, positioning himself to confront Carl Fredricksen upon arrival.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz spots Carl's house approaching and initiates the ramp deployment while ordering heightened security, marking the start of the final confrontation sequence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To secure Kevin and eliminate any threat from Fredricksen upon his arrival on the dirigible.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The ramp begins to open beneath Russell, isolating him and cutting off immediate rescue, while guards are ordered to act against Fredricksen.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell remains trapped and vulnerable, with no clear means of escape or communication to warn Fredricksen.
evidence_quotes: Alpha! Fredricksen's coming back.
Muntz pulls the switch and walks out of the room.
Nice talking with you.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charles Muntz assumes direct control of the dirigible to personally confront Fredricksen, marking his transition from a commanding antagonist to an active pursuer.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz takes the wheel of the dirigible and initiates a personal aerial pursuit of Fredricksen, escalating the conflict to a direct confrontation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To locate and confront Carl Fredricksen, whom he sees as a threat to his legacy and mission.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz is now actively engaged in the chase, personally at the helm, intensifying the immediate danger to Fredricksen and the stakes of the conflict.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The outcome of the aerial pursuit and whether Muntz will succeed in capturing or stopping Fredricksen remains uncertain.
evidence_quotes: Muntz enters the cockpit and takes the wheel.
Where are you, Fredricksen?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charles Muntz learns that Carl has the bird and is aboard the dirigible, shattering his sense of control.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Maintain exclusive possession of the bird and complete his mission unchallenged.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Faced with the sudden threat of losing the bird to Carl, Muntz shifts from calm authority to alarmed urgency.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to regain control of the bird and eliminate Carl's interference.
evidence_quotes: What? Calm down! One at a time!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charles Muntz is the antagonist who shifts from commanding his forces remotely to directly engaging the protagonists upon Russell's unexpected intrusion.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz transitions from reactive command to active aggression by ordering the immediate attack on Carl's house upon realizing the protagonists have infiltrated his dirigible.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To capture the rare bird and eliminate any threats to his mission by destroying the house and stopping the intruders.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz initiates offensive action, escalating the conflict by mobilizing his airship forces to destroy the house and recapture the bird.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The protagonists remain at large and in possession of the bird, posing an ongoing threat to Muntz's mission.
evidence_quotes: Does anyone know where they are?!?!
Grey leader! Take down the house!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Antagonist confronting the protagonists directly to capture the bird and prevent their escape.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz abandons stealth and pretense, launching a violent physical attack on Carl and the bird.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Capture the bird and maintain control over his legacy and reputation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The confrontation escalates into open conflict, with Muntz resorting to force and temporarily separating the group.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Muntz's obsession with the bird remains unfulfilled, and the protagonists continue to evade him despite his aggression.
evidence_quotes: Muntz kicks Dug loose and out the door. He slams it shut.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz's obsession culminates in a violent, unhinged assault on Carl, revealing his complete moral collapse and willingness to kill for the bird.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To capture Kevin the bird at any cost, even murder, to reclaim his lost honor and prove his life's work.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz gains temporary physical dominance over Carl and prepares to deliver a fatal blow, fully embracing his role as a villain.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Muntz will succeed in killing Carl and capturing Kevin, leaving the bird's fate and Muntz's ultimate downfall still in suspense.
evidence_quotes: Muntz advances on Carl, who struggles to protect the bird.
Muntz swings his sword at Carl but misses. Carl uses his
Muntz smashes his trophy collection as he swings for Carl.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: antagonist confronting Carl in the trophy room aboard the dirigible
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz physically attacks Carl after the dirigible's violent tilt, marking the final descent into open combat between the two.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz aims to capture Kevin and eliminate Carl as an obstacle to his mission.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The confrontation escalates to life-threatening violence, with Carl nearly falling from the dirigible and Muntz advancing aggressively.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The immediate threat of Muntz's assault remains unresolved, with Carl fighting to survive and protect Kevin.
evidence_quotes: Muntz comes at Carl.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: pursuing Carl up the side of the dirigible
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz escalates the chase by following Carl outside the dirigible, advancing the confrontation into a more dangerous and exposed environment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to capture Kevin and stop Carl from escaping with him
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: the pursuit continues on the exterior of the dirigible, placing both Muntz and Carl at greater physical risk
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Muntz will succeed in capturing Kevin or be thwarted by the extreme conditions and Carl's resistance
evidence_quotes: Muntz follows up.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: antagonist in direct physical confrontation with Carl during the climax of his mission
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz suffers a critical physical defeat when Carl knocks him off the ladder, breaking his control over the situation and ending his ability to stop Carl's escape
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz aims to prevent Carl from escaping with Kevin and reclaim the bird to restore his reputation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz is incapacitated and forced to descend the ladder, losing both Kevin and the upper hand in the confrontation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Muntz survives but his legacy and mission remain irreparably collapsed, with no clear path to redemption or recovery
evidence_quotes: Muntz grabs Carl's foot.
Muntz descends the ladder.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: antagonist confronting the protagonists on the dirigible
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz violently intervenes by shooting the balloon strings, causing the house to crash onto the dirigible and nearly fall off the edge, directly endangering Carl, Russell, Dug, and Kevin.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz aims to capture Kevin at any cost, driven by his obsession to validate his reputation and prove his discovery of the bird.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The house is critically destabilized, teetering on the edge of the dirigible's tail fin, with Carl barely holding it in place while Russell, Dug, and Kevin are trapped inside under Muntz's assault.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Muntz remains at large and in control of the dirigible, actively trying to break into the house to seize Kevin, creating immediate danger with no clear escape for the protagonists.
evidence_quotes: BLAM! Muntz appears on top of the dirigible with a rifle!
A bullet blasts through Carl's balloon strings.
Muntz POUNDS on the door with the butt of the rifle.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: active antagonist advancing toward the house to capture Kevin
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz forcefully breaches the house by kicking open the door, escalating the immediate threat to Kevin and the protagonists
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz remains fully committed to capturing Kevin to validate his reputation and mission
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The protagonists are now in direct confrontation with Muntz, triggering Carl's urgent instructions to Russell to protect Kevin
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Muntz will succeed in capturing Kevin or be thwarted by Carl and Russell remains uncertain
evidence_quotes: Muntz KICKS the door.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: antagonist entering the house and aiming his rifle at Kevin
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Muntz forcibly enters Carl's house and takes aim at Kevin with his rifle, marking the peak of his pursuit
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz is on the verge of capturing Kevin, the living proof he needs to restore his reputation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Kevin is cornered and in immediate danger, with Muntz's weapon trained directly on her
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Muntz will succeed in shooting Kevin or be stopped in time
evidence_quotes: Through the window, Carl yells out.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: desperate antagonist attempting to capture Kevin
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After Kevin knocks the rifle from his hands, Muntz lunges in a failed attempt to regain control, marking a turning point in his physical dominance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To capture Kevin and complete his mission of proving the bird's existence
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz loses both his weapon and tactical advantage, shifting from controlled aggressor to frantic pursuer.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Muntz's obsession remains unfulfilled, and his failure to secure Kevin jeopardizes his life's work.
evidence_quotes: Muntz lunges after them.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Antagonist confronting the protagonists during the climax; attempts to reclaim Kevin but is thwarted by his own balloon system.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charles Muntz falls to his death after being entangled by his own balloon strings while trying to stop the protagonists from escaping with Kevin.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To capture Kevin and prove his scientific claims to the world, thereby restoring his reputation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: His goal is permanently unfulfilled; his obsession ends in failure and death.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: None — Muntz's arc concludes definitively with his death, leaving no possibility for future resolution or redemption.
evidence_quotes: Muntz leaps after them.
But balloon strings tangle around his leg and pull him back.
He falls.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: indirect
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charles Muntz's dirigible, a symbol of his authority and mission, is now under the control of Dug and the dogs, marking the definitive end of his influence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Muntz's goal of capturing Kevin was central to his legacy and operation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: His goal is abandoned and reversed, as the dogs who once served him now celebrate its failure and align with the protagonists.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Posthumous collapse of his operation and ideology.
evidence_quotes: Muntz's dirigible ascends into the sky.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Russell begins as a determined but lonely boy chasing validation through a badge, persistently seeking Carl's approval despite rejection, yet evolves through defiance, danger, and moral courage into a self-assured adventurer who saves lives and earns genuine connection; by the journey's end, he no longer needs the badge he once craved, having transformed from a needy outsider into a trusted equal, finally receiving the paternal recognition he longed for not from his father, but from the man who became his family.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is initiating contact with Carl as part of his goal to earn the Assisting the Elderly badge, positioning himself as a persistent and motivated child scout.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell makes first contact with Carl, persistently offering assistance despite repeated rejections, establishing the central dynamic between the two characters.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell aims to assist an elderly person to earn his Assisting the Elderly badge, and he explicitly pursues this goal by approaching Carl.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl rejects Russell's offers and closes the door, leaving Russell physically rebuffed but emotionally undeterred.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell remains determined to help Carl despite the clear rejection, indicating that his need for validation through the badge is stronger than the immediate dismissal.
evidence_quotes: I could help you cross the street.
I could help you cross your yard.
I could help you cross your... porch?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell persists in his goal to assist Carl in order to earn his Assisting the Elderly badge, positioning himself as a determined and emotionally invested boy scout.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell refuses to be dismissed by Carl, physically blocking the door and revealing his deeper emotional motivation: wanting his father to attend his Senior Wilderness Explorer ceremony.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell's goal remains obtaining the Assisting the Elderly badge to become a Senior Wilderness Explorer, now explicitly tied to his desire for paternal recognition and presence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl manipulates Russell into leaving by inventing a fake task about catching a snipe, temporarily dismissing him but inadvertently setting up future interaction and unintended consequences.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell is unaware he has been tricked and is now chasing a nonexistent creature, leaving his goal unfulfilled and his role in assisting Carl unresolved.
evidence_quotes: Me! Me! I'll do it!
Two blocks down! Got it!
Sniiiipe! Here Snipey Snipey...

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: persistent intruder seeking shelter
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell defies Carl's initial refusal and gains entry into the house, marking the beginning of their forced cohabitation and relationship.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell seeks safety and connection after failing to find a snipe and becoming trapped under the porch.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell successfully enters Carl's house, physically breaking into his isolated world and establishing an irreversible presence.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Carl's reluctant acceptance suggests ongoing tension between his desire for solitude and Russell's need for companionship and guidance.
evidence_quotes: Please let me in?
Russell runs into the house past Carl.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell confidently presents his GPS as a reliable tool for navigating to Paradise Falls, but his overenthusiastic gesture causes him to accidentally throw it out the window, resulting in its permanent loss.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell aims to prove his competence and usefulness to Carl by demonstrating his navigational knowledge and equipment.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The GPS is destroyed in the fall or lost beyond recovery, eliminating a key technological aid for locating Paradise Falls and increasing the difficulty of the journey.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Without the GPS, Carl and Russell must now rely on alternative, less precise methods to navigate to Paradise Falls, introducing uncertainty and greater risk to their mission.
evidence_quotes: With this baby, we'll never be lost!
Russell gestures, tossing the unit out the window.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell saves Carl from falling off a cliff and shifts from being a passive sidekick to an active, life-saving partner, establishing mutual dependence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell aims to assist Carl and earn his badge by proving his usefulness on the journey.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell successfully prevents Carl's death, helps stabilize their situation, and proposes a new plan to move the house toward Paradise Falls, redefining his role in the mission.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell is physically exhausted and unable to hoist Carl up the hose, leaving them stranded at the cliff edge and dependent on finding another solution.
evidence_quotes: Whoah!
Okay!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell discovers mysterious tracks, follows them, and makes first contact with a large, elusive creature he believes is a 'giant snipe,' initiating a playful and compassionate interaction centered on sharing chocolate.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell's initial goal is to complete his Wilderness Explorer badge by finding a snipe, a task rooted in duty and achievement.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: His goal transforms into forming a genuine emotional connection with the creature, reflecting curiosity, care, and wonder rather than mere task completion.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The true nature and intentions of the creature remain unknown, creating mild suspense and foreshadowing future developments in their relationship.
evidence_quotes: Huh? Tracks?!
Snipe!
...giant snipe!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Active participant in discovering Kevin's location and learning she has babies
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell discovers that Kevin is a female bird with offspring, which redefines her prior actions as maternal care rather than random behavior
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Reinforces commitment to helping Kevin reunite with her young
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell shifts from concern over Kevin's disappearance to urgent determination to ensure her safe return to her babies
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tension arises between Russell's desire to help Kevin reunite with her babies and Carl's urgency to continue their journey, risking Kevin's safety
evidence_quotes: Kevin! Dug, find Kevin!
Oh, look! There he is.
Her babies! Kevin's a girl?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional catalyst and moral challenger
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell witnesses Kevin's abduction by Muntz and confronts Carl, accusing him of betrayal, which marks a breakdown in their trust and a shift in Russell's role from passive companion to active resister.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell's goal evolves from helping Kevin as part of a shared journey to actively fighting to rescue her, now seeing her capture as a personal moral imperative.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell is emotionally devastated and angry, now committed to the rescue mission despite Carl's emotional withdrawal and hostility.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The fractured relationship between Russell and Carl remains unresolved, with Carl rejecting responsibility and Russell holding him accountable for Kevin's capture.
evidence_quotes: No!
Kevin!
Let her go! Stop!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell rejects his Wilderness Explorer sash, symbolizing his emotional break from the goal of earning the 'Assistance to the Elderly' badge due to feeling unacknowledged by Carl.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell abandons his external goal of earning the badge, shifting focus to his unmet emotional need for recognition and connection.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell is isolated and withdrawn, sitting alone on a rock, while Carl remains unaware of the emotional rift he has caused.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The lack of acknowledgment from Carl creates a deep emotional disconnect, leaving Russell feeling invisible and unvalued.
evidence_quotes: Behind him, Russell throws his sash on the ground.
I don't want this anymore.
Russell walks away. He sits on a rock, alone.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell defies Carl's authority by using balloons and a leaf blower to float away toward the dirigible, asserting his independence and moral conviction to rescue Kevin.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell reaffirms his commitment to help Kevin, prioritizing his personal sense of duty over obedience to Carl.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell successfully initiates his solo journey into the sky, becoming an autonomous agent in the mission and shifting from sidekick to independent protagonist.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell's plan is risky and untested, leaving uncertainty about his ability to survive the flight, reach the dirigible, or successfully rescue Kevin without adult supervision.
evidence_quotes: Russell fires up a LEAF BLOWER and steers himself off into
A shadow crosses Carl. He looks up to see Russell floating
Russell?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is the immediate focus of Carl's attention and the source of the knocking, implying his imminent return or presence.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Carl's startled reaction and immediate vocalization of Russell's name indicate a significant emotional shift, highlighting Russell's critical role in Carl's internal transformation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl becomes alert and emotionally engaged upon hearing the knock, suggesting a transition from isolation to openness, prompted by the expectation of Russell's presence.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: It remains unclear whether Russell is actually at the door or if Carl's response is driven by hope or guilt, leaving his physical and emotional safety uncertain.
evidence_quotes: KNOCK KNOCK KNOCK. Carl's eyes go wide.
Russell?

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is the active protagonist in this scene, solely responsible for advancing the rescue mission by piloting himself through the sky toward the dirigible to save Kevin.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell demonstrates full agency and determination as he navigates the aerial environment, marking a pivotal moment in his heroic journey.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To reach the dirigible and rescue Kevin before it's too late.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell is making measurable progress toward the dirigible, closing the physical gap between himself and the rescue objective.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The environmental risks of flying through open sky and the unknown conditions aboard the dirigible remain threats to the mission's success.
evidence_quotes: Russell steers through the clouds toward the DIRIGIBLE.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell overcomes physical obstacles to enter the dirigible, marking a transition from external pursuit to direct confrontation with the antagonist.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell aims to enter the dirigible to rescue Kevin and confront Muntz.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell successfully gains entry into the dirigible, positioning himself inside the antagonist's domain.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The immediate danger of the dirigible's unstable environment and the impending confrontation with Muntz remain unresolved.
evidence_quotes: Russell steers wobbly toward an open dirigible window.
He tries again and TUMBLES through the window.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is in the foreground, directly confronting Muntz while captured, marking a pivotal shift in his arc and relationship with Carl.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell is captured by Muntz and explicitly rejects Carl as a friend, signaling a rupture in their bond and a personal turning point.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell's goal shifts from assisting Carl to resisting Muntz and protecting Kevin, asserting independence through defiance.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell is physically restrained and removed by Muntz, heightening the danger and setting the stage for Carl's return.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The breakdown of trust between Russell and Carl remains unresolved, with emotional consequences pending.
evidence_quotes: Let me go!
He's not my friend anymore.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell transitions from verbal resistance to facing immediate physical danger as the ramp opens beneath him, exposing him to the open sky.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To resist capture and confront Muntz.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: To survive and escape as the ramp opens, placing him in imminent peril.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell is trapped on an opening ramp with no clear means of escape, and Carl's arrival may not be in time to save him.
evidence_quotes: Let me go!
Nice talking with you.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is in immediate peril, tied to a chair sliding toward the edge of the dirigible ramp, prompting Carl's rescue mission.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell's life is in immediate danger as he slides helplessly toward the edge of the lowering ramp, forcing Carl to abandon his original objective and initiate a rescue.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Survival and rescue from imminent fall from the dirigible.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl interrupts his mission to prioritize Russell's safety, marking a shift from solitary purpose to protective guardianship.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Russell can be saved before the chair goes over the edge remains uncertain at the scene's end.
evidence_quotes: Carl steers toward the cockpit. He dons Russell's sash, then
He hears SCREAMING. It's Russell! Tied to his chair, he
Russell!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell's accidental crash into the dirigible's cockpit exposes his presence and alerts Muntz to the house's location, shifting him from hidden stowaway to active target.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell's unintended impact on the cockpit window reveals the house's position to Muntz, who immediately orders an attack.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Muntz is now aware of Russell and the house's presence, escalating the threat level and initiating direct confrontation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell remains physically exposed and vulnerable, with no clear means of escape from Muntz's imminent attack.
evidence_quotes: On cue, Russell SPLATS into the dashboard window of the
Muntz stares, slack-jawed.
Whoooaaah!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is directly endangered during a military-style strafing attack on the house carried out by dog-piloted biplanes.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell is directly endangered during a military-style strafing attack on the house carried out by dog-piloted biplanes.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell survives the initial assault but remains in extreme physical danger, suspended beneath the attacked house.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell has no means of immediate escape or defense while dangling defenselessly during the ongoing attack.
evidence_quotes: The biplanes fire and strafe Carl's house with darts.
Russell SCREAMS, swaying beneath it on the hose.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell transitions from a struggling, fearful boy to an active hero, responding to Carl's call and using cleverness to outmaneuver the dog-piloted biplanes.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell overcomes physical exhaustion and fear, climbs the hose under fire, and uses the dogs' conditioned response to 'squirrel' to disable the attacking planes.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To reach safety and help defend Carl from Muntz and the biplanes.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell successfully reaches the house, disables the biplanes by tricking the dog pilots, and proves himself as a courageous and resourceful partner to Carl.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Muntz remains a threat as he continues his pursuit of Carl aboard the dirigible.
evidence_quotes: I can't do it!
Carl calls for help: the WILDERNESS EXPLORER CALL.
You leave Mr. Fredricksen alone!
The planes dive, crash into each other.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell actively responds to Carl's call for help by steering the house onto the dirigible, directly enabling the rescue of Carl and Kevin, and becomes a key participant in the group's survival during Muntz's attack.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell successfully maneuvers the house onto the dirigible, allowing Carl and the others to regroup, but the house is then shot and left teetering on the edge, placing Russell and the others in immediate peril.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To reach and rescue Carl and Kevin by landing the house safely on the dirigible
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The house is critically damaged and perched precariously on the dirigible's tail fin, with Russell, Dug, and Kevin trapped inside as Muntz advances aggressively.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell and the others are cornered and in imminent danger as Muntz attacks and the house remains on the verge of falling, while Carl struggles to hold it in place.
evidence_quotes: Russell steers the house toward Carl.
Russell, Dug and Kevin run toward the front porch.
Russell and others duck inside and slam the door.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell bonds with Carl through shared relief and joy after their narrow escape, marking a deep emotional connection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell achieves his emotional goal of earning Carl's paternal approval, symbolizing the fulfillment of his 'Assisting the Elderly' badge.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Carl physically affirms their bond by putting his arm around Russell, signifying a familial relationship and emotional closure.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Emotional release after intense physical danger.
evidence_quotes: That was cool!
Carl smiles. He puts his arm around Russell.
You know, it's just a house.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: foreground
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell emotionally matures by saying goodbye to Kevin and the baby birds, accepting that they belong in the wild.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell desires to keep the birds, reflecting his attachment and longing for connection.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: He lets go of the birds, embracing the value of their freedom and demonstrating empathy and responsibility.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell's wish to keep one of the birds highlights his ongoing need for companionship.
evidence_quotes: I wish I could keep one.
Bye Kevin!

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is now an equal partner and co-adventurer with Carl, no longer seeking validation but sharing in leadership.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell and Carl don identical flight helmets, symbolizing their unity and Russell's full acceptance as a capable partner.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell has transitioned from a boy seeking merit badges to a confident adventurer embraced as an equal.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell and Carl prepare to fly the dirigible together, marking the beginning of a new phase in their journey and relationship.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Transition from crisis to a new beginning.
evidence_quotes: Carl gives a thumbs up.

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Russell is the focal recipient of recognition in the ceremony, transitioning from isolation to familial belonging through Carl's intervention.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Russell, initially alone and vulnerable when his father is absent, receives emotional validation as Carl steps in to award him the Ellie Badge, symbolizing paternal approval and closure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Russell's enduring emotional goal of gaining recognition and connection from a father figure is symbolically fulfilled.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Russell achieves a sense of belonging and emotional completion, visibly proud and embraced by both Carl and his mother.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Russell experiences social and emotional pressure when the Camp Master notices his father is absent, creating vulnerability.
evidence_quotes: Russell is tanned and dirty. And alone.
It's Ellie's GRAPE SODA PIN.
